openmeter/billing/worker/subscriptionsyn.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing subscriptionsync.Adapter and SyncStateAdapter — persists SubscriptionBillingSyncState records (upsert, get, invalidate) with full ctx-bound transaction support via entutils.TransactingRepo. Pure data access; no orchestration.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping all DB writes
patterns.description: Every DB-touching method calls entutils.TransactingRepo or TransactingRepoWithNoValue so the ctx-bound transaction is honored rather than falling off to the raw client.
patterns.example: entutils.TransactingRepoWithNoValue(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) error { return tx.db.SubscriptionBillingSyncState.Delete()... })

patterns.name: Config + Validate constructor
patterns.description: New takes a Config, calls config.Validate() accumulating errors via errors.Join, and returns (Interface, error).
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (subscriptionsync.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Tx / WithTx / Self triad
patterns.description: Implement TxCreator: Tx() starts a hijacked tx, WithTx() rebinds the adapter to a TxDriver, Self() returns self; required for TransactingRepo.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txDb := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txDb.Client()} }

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: Compile-time assertion at the top of each implementation file catches interface drift early.

patterns.name: UTC normalization on time fields
patterns.description: All DB time values are normalized to UTC via .UTC() / lo.ToPtr(t.UTC()) to prevent timezone drift.

patterns.name: Upsert with OnConflictColumns
patterns.description: Upsert uses Ent's OnConflictColumns(...).UpdateXxx() keyed on (subscription_id, namespace) to make UpsertSyncState idempotent.

key_file_guides.role: Config, adapter struct, New constructor, Tx/WithTx/Self triad — entry point for adapter construction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding business logic here; all logic belongs in syncstate.go or domain files.

key_file_guides.role: Implements SyncStateAdapter: InvalidateSyncState, GetSyncStates, UpsertSyncState over the SubscriptionBillingSyncState entity.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Forgetting the TransactingRepo wrapper bypasses ctx-bound transactions; missing UTC() normalization on time fields.

anti_patterns: Calling tx.db directly without wrapping in entutils.TransactingRepo/TransactingRepoWithNoValue.
Storing or returning time values without .UTC() normalization.
Adding domain or sync orchestration logic into the adapter (belongs in the service layer).
Skipping the compile-time interface assertion var _ Interface = (*adapter)(nil).
Constructing the adapter without a Config.Validate() call.

decisions.decision: Adapter is scoped exclusively to SubscriptionBillingSyncState persistence; no subscription/invoice business logic.
decisions.rationale: Separation of concerns: the service layer owns orchestration, the adapter owns data access, keeping it independently testable.

decisions.decision: TransactingRepo is used even for single-statement operations.
decisions.rationale: Callers may already have a transaction in ctx; rebinding ensures atomicity with surrounding operations and prevents partial writes.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new adapter method that writes to DB

openmeter/billing/worker/subscriptionsyn.purpose: Crash-recovery reconciler that periodically re-drives subscription-to-billing sync for subscriptions that may have missed event-driven processing. Iterates active subscriptions in paginated windows and calls SynchronizeSubscription for each eligible entry. Service-interface only; no DB access.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate + named constructor
patterns.description: ReconcilerConfig holds all dependencies; Validate() checks each for nil; NewReconciler validates before constructing and returns (*Reconciler, error).
patterns.example: func NewReconciler(config ReconcilerConfig) (*Reconciler, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Paginated window scan with defaultWindowSize
patterns.description: ListSubscriptions iterates pages of 10,000 via pagination.Page until an empty page returns, preventing unbounded memory usage.
patterns.example: for { subs, _ := r.subscriptionService.List(ctx, subscription.ListSubscriptionsInput{Page: pagination.Page{PageNumber: pageIndex, PageSize: defaultWindowSize}}); if len(subs.Items) == 0 { break }; pageIndex++ }

patterns.name: SyncState-gated skipping logic
patterns.description: All() skips subscriptions where SyncState.HasBillables is false or NextSyncAfter is nil/in the future, avoiding unnecessary re-sync when the state machine is quiescent.

patterns.name: errors.Join for non-fatal per-item errors
patterns.description: All() accumulates per-subscription errors via errors.Join, logging each but continuing so one failure does not abort the batch.
patterns.example: outErr = errors.Join(outErr, fmt.Errorf("failed to reconcile subscription: %w", err))

patterns.name: clock.Now() for testable time
patterns.description: Use clock.Now() (not time.Now()) when computing lookback windows and comparing NextSyncAfter to enable clock injection in tests.

key_file_guides.role: Entire reconciler: config, ListSubscriptions (paginated), ReconcileSubscription (single), All (batch with skip logic). Delegates to subscriptionsync.Service and subscription.Service; no DB access.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Calling time.Now() directly instead of clock.Now(); adding DB access (route through services); missing deleted-customer guard in ReconcileSubscription.

anti_patterns: Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() — breaks time-based tests.
Loading all subscriptions into memory without pagination (respect defaultWindowSize).
Aborting the entire batch on a single error instead of accumulating with errors.Join.
Adding Ent/DB imports — this layer must remain service-interface-only.
Removing the deleted-customer guard in ReconcileSubscription without equivalent replacement.

decisions.decision: Reconciler owns no DB adapter; it orchestrates exclusively through service interfaces.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the reconciler testable with mocks and decoupled from persistence; transactionality is managed by the service layer.

decisions.decision: Force flag bypasses SyncState-based skipping for manual/admin full re-sync.
decisions.rationale: Administrators need to force reconciliation during incident recovery without clearing sync state first.

code_examples.scenario: A reconciler method listing and processing subscriptions in a paginated batch
